new_exact:

source: PDF visual rendering (page 3) shows [¶2] label present before this paragraph; DB text is missing the label
note: Missing paragraph marker [¶2]; this paragraph is unnumbered in the DB but should be numbered. The section marker Ι itself is correct (Greek iota used in opinion headings).

source: Unicode analysis: Ι is Greek capital iota (U+0399), should be Latin capital I (U+0049); ND opinions use Latin letters for section markers
note: Two instances (lines 43 and 215): Greek iota substituted for Latin I in section markers. However, line 43 marks the start of the main opinion's section I, and rendering confirms it should be 'I'.

source: Unicode analysis: В is Cyrillic capital VE (U+0412), should be Latin Roman numeral IV; standard section numbering in ND opinions uses I, II, III, IV
note: Four instances (lines 99, 159, 243, 419): Cyrillic В substituted for Roman numeral IV in section markers

source: PDF page 3, section heading between [¶6] and [¶7]
note: Greek capital pi (U+03A0) misrecognized as Roman numeral II. Confirmed via PDF inspection showing Latin 'II' in heading position.

source: PDF page 5, section heading between [¶10] and [¶11]
note: Cyrillic capital B (U+0412) misrecognized as section marker. PDF shows Latin 'B' as subsection heading.

source: Source PDF shows compact four-dot ellipsis '....' at Rule 11(c)(5) quotation block end, indicating spaced form should be '. . . .' not '. . . . .'
evidence: DB text has spaced ellipsis with 5 dots/spaces before '(Emphasis added.)'. Source PDF shows standard four-dot form, consistent with other spaced ellipses in opinion (e.g., in Ostafin quote: '[defendant] . . . .' and 'sentence to . . . uphold').
note: Ingestion artifact: excessive spacing in ellipsis. Correction aligns with ND opinion ellipsis style.
